title: Lasiochloa adscendens
intro: Lasiochloa adscendens is a plant species commonly known as 'Wild Oat' or 'Panic Grass', belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lasiochloa adscendens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as infusions and decoctions.
taxonomy: Lasiochloa adscendens is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. This family is commonly referred to as the grass family. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. Lasiochloa adscendens is a type of grass that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Lasiochloa adscendens is further refined at the genus and species levels.
